blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: She does. She always has. Even back in college — she'd notice things nobody else caught. Small things.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: It used to drive people a little crazy actually. Like you'd say something offhand and two weeks later she'd reference it and you'd think — how did you hold onto that?

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Memory like that is usually tied to something specific.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Caring a lot about something. Or someone.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: She's had a rough couple years. Not anything dramatic — just quiet hard. The kind you don't talk about.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I just want this weekend to be good for her. For all of us. It's been a while since we were just — us. No distance. No life stuff in the way.
He meant every word of it. That was the part that was hard to file away.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: There he is. I knew you'd be out here.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: I said I'm good. Drop it.

blocks.prompt: Julian is watching this. What does he read?

options.label: Cade is carrying something he doesn't want Marcus to see.
He just wants his space. Some people don't do mornings.
He's not just private. He's scared of something.
